Gate Runner→

Fly real aircraft round a winding circuit of rings. Your true wingspan is the hitbox.

    🚧 Website under construction — we’re still building this site. 🚧

    Aircraft — for your information.

    Every giant of the sky — measured, sourced, and drawn to true scale. No stock photos. No guesswork.

      84.00 mlongest ever 97.54 mwidest wings Mach 3.02fastest giant 68 + 50aircraft & records
      See the Top 50 → Browse the fleet
      The fleet

      Aircraft, page by page

      Every silhouette below is sized by real wingspan — the bigger the aircraft, the bigger it draws. Each page carries the full sourced spec table, every variant, and every operator.

      Filter
      Sort
      Grid

      Hughes H-4 Hercules

      Hughes · Preserved — flew once, 1947

      Length66.65 m
      Wingspan97.54 m
      Height24.18 m
      MTOW180,000 kg
      Full specification →

      Antonov An-225 Mriya

      Antonov · Destroyed (2022) — one built

      Length84.00 m
      Wingspan88.40 m
      Height18.10 m
      MTOW640,000 kg
      Full specification →

      Airbus A380

      Airbus · Out of production (2021) — in service

      Length72.72 m
      Wingspan79.75 m
      Height24.09 m
      MTOW575,000 kg
      Full specification →

      Length69.10 m
      Wingspan73.30 m
      Height20.78 m
      MTOW402,000 kg
      Full specification →

      Boeing · In certification — deliveries expected 2027

      Wingspan (extended)71.75 m
      Wingspan (folded)64.85 m
      Height19.68 m

      Airlines

      Fleets, airline by airline

      Grid
      EK

      Emirates

      United Arab Emirates · None (independent)

      The world's largest international airline — an all-widebody fleet built on the A380 and 777.

      See the fleet →
      BA

      British Airways

      United Kingdom · oneworld

      The UK flag carrier — one of the few airlines flying the A380, 777 and A350 side by side.

      See the fleet →
      QR

      Qatar Airways

      Qatar · oneworld

      A350 launch operator and one of the world's largest widebody fleets, split across Airbus and Boeing.

      See the fleet →
      FR

      Ryanair

      Ireland · None (independent)

      Europe's biggest airline by passengers — a single-type all-Boeing 737 operation.

      See the fleet →
      SQ

      Singapore Airlines

      Singapore · Star Alliance

      The A380's launch operator — first airline in the world to fly the superjumbo, in October 2007.

      See the fleet →
      QF

      Qantas

      Australia · oneworld

      The Australian flag carrier — a century old, flying the A380 on the Kangaroo Route and trans-Pacific trunk lines.

      See the fleet →
      EY

      Etihad Airways

      United Arab Emirates · None (independent)

      Abu Dhabi's flag carrier — parked its entire A380 fleet in the pandemic, then brought it back.

      See the fleet →
      LH

      Lufthansa

      Germany · Star Alliance

      Germany's flag carrier and Europe's largest airline group — flying its A380s from Munich.

      See the fleet →
      KE

      Korean Air

      South Korea · SkyTeam

      South Korea's flag carrier — A380s and one of Asia's biggest 777 fleets, now absorbing Asiana.

      See the fleet →
      OZ

      Asiana Airlines

      South Korea · Star Alliance

      Seoul's second superjumbo operator — flying A380s on Asian trunk routes while merging into Korean Air.

      See the fleet →
      NH

      All Nippon Airways

      Japan · Star Alliance

      Japan's largest airline — the last airline ever to introduce the A380, with three sea-turtle Flying Honu jets.

      See the fleet →
      UA

      United Airlines

      United States · Star Alliance

      The largest airline fleet in the world, and the only US carrier flying all three 787 variants.

      See the fleet →
      AA

      American Airlines

      United States · oneworld

      One of the biggest airlines in the world by any measure — and the largest carrier in the oneworld alliance.

      See the fleet →
      DL

      Delta Air Lines

      United States · SkyTeam

      America's oldest operating airline, flying since 1925 — and the industry's revenue heavyweight.

      See the fleet →
      CA

      Air China

      China · Star Alliance

      China's flag carrier — the only airline that flies the national flag on its tail.

      See the fleet →
      CZ

      China Southern Airlines

      China · No alliance

      The largest fleet in Asia — 600-plus aircraft radiating out of Guangzhou.

      See the fleet →
      MU

      China Eastern Airlines

      China · SkyTeam

      The Shanghai giant — and global launch operator of China's own C919.

      See the fleet →
      TK

      Turkish Airlines

      Türkiye · Star Alliance

      Flies to more countries than any other airline on Earth, all through Istanbul.

      See the fleet →
      CX

      Cathay Pacific

      Hong Kong · oneworld

      Hong Kong's flag carrier — a long-haul specialist built around one of aviation's great hubs.

      See the fleet →
      KL

      KLM

      Netherlands · SkyTeam

      The oldest airline in the world still flying under its original name — airborne since 1919.

      See the fleet →
      AC

      Air Canada

      Canada · Star Alliance

      Canada's flag carrier, linking six continents through Toronto and Vancouver.

      See the fleet →
      AF

      Air France

      France · SkyTeam

      The French flag carrier — one half of Europe's largest long-haul airline group.

      See the fleet →
      JL

      Japan Airlines

      Japan · oneworld

      Japan's flag carrier — rebuilt into one of the most reliable long-haul operators anywhere.

      See the fleet →
      FX

      FedEx Express

      United States · Cargo

      The largest cargo airline on the planet — the overnight backbone of world freight.

      See the fleet →
      5X

      UPS Airlines

      United States · Cargo

      The world's number-two cargo carrier — and the largest operator of the 747-8 Freighter.

      See the fleet →
      5Y

      Atlas Air

      United States · Cargo

      The world's largest 747 operator — and the airline that flew home the very last 747 ever built.

      See the fleet →
      Novelty

      Bring back Concorde

      Twenty-three years ago, the fastest way across the Atlantic retired — and nothing replaced it.

      A petition with absolutely no legal force, a slide-to-verify gate, one signature per browser, and a SHA-256 receipt for every name. Purely because we miss it.

      Sign the petition →Is supersonic coming back?
      Browse

      By maker, or by kind

      Manufacturers

      Twelve builders, from Airbus and Boeing to Antonov, Tupolev, Hughes and Bristol — each with its full line-up.

      All manufacturers →

      Aircraft types

      Widebody, narrowbody, freighter, military transport, bomber, supersonic, trijet, propliner — every category explained.

      All types →

      The blog

      Long-form writing on the aircraft we cover — sourced, dated, and honest about what nobody knows yet.

      Read the blog →

      Explained

      MTOW, ETOPS, range versus payload, winglets, turbofans — the ideas behind every spec table.

      Start reading →
      For travellers

      Before you fly

      Scared of flying?

      The fear is usually a fear of the unknown. Learn to think like a pilot, understand every noise and movement, and let the engineering put you at ease.

      Feel calmer →

      Fly better for less

      How the cabins really differ, the evergreen tricks for cheaper flights, and how points can turn an economy budget into a business-class seat.

      Fly smarter →

      The hangar shop

      A short, honest shelf for people who love flying machines — LEGO sets, die-cast models, spotter tech and flight-sim kit. Picked on merit, never paid placement.

      Browse the shelf →
      Beat the fear Fly for less
      Now live

      The Scale Engine

      Any two aircraft, rendered at true relative scale — next to a 1.8 m person, a double-decker bus and a Boeing 737. Honest proportions, always.

      Open the compare tool →See all four record boards
      B737aircraft.fyi

      The visual reference for aircraft. Sourced spec tables, every operator, and — soon — true-scale comparison of anything that flies.

      ships.fyi luck.fyi calculate.to

      Aircraft

      • Hughes H-4 Hercules
      • Antonov An-225 Mriya
      • Airbus A380
      • Antonov An-124 Ruslan
      • Boeing 777X

      About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
      © 2026 aircraft.fyi
